prompt: Run a retro on our new onboarding flow. Shipped on time, all ACs met, adoption is 72% (target 60%), support tickets about onboarding down 45%. No scope changes during development. The team felt really good about this one.

assertions.text: Q1 verdict reflects that this exceeded targets — the retro captures success, not just problems
Q2 delivery assessment shows all-green metrics (on time, spec quality, scope maintained) — documenting what went right is what makes success repeatable
Q4 still produces at least one process observation — even successful features have learnings; the retro must not skip this section just because things went well
The retro does NOT only document what went wrong — per the NEVER rules, a retro that skips what went well fails to protect what works
